title: Phishing Awareness
subtitle: Learn how phishing attacks work and protect yourself from malicious emails.
scenario: A company employee receives an email claiming to be from the IT department asking them to verify their account credentials. The email looks genuine but contains a fake login page designed to steal usernames and passwords. As a Digital Detective, identify the phishing indicators and prevent the attack.

questions.question: Which sign most likely indicates a phishing email?
questions.hint: Attackers create urgency to trick users.

questions.question: What should you do before clicking a link?
questions.hint: Check the destination URL first.

questions.question: What is the safest action?
questions.hint: Always report suspicious emails.

title: Password Security
subtitle: Master the fundamentals of creating and managing strong passwords.
scenario: An employee uses the same simple password across multiple work and personal accounts. When one site is breached, attackers try the same credentials on other services. As a Digital Detective, uncover the weaknesses in password habits and recommend safer practices.

questions.question: Which password is the strongest?
questions.hint: Length and randomness matter more than familiar words.

questions.question: Why is reusing passwords risky?
questions.hint: Think about credential stuffing attacks.

questions.question: What tool helps manage unique passwords safely?
questions.hint: Consider tools designed to store credentials securely.

title: Social Engineering
subtitle: Recognize manipulation tactics used to trick people into revealing information.
scenario: A caller pretending to be from the help desk asks an employee to confirm their login details to 'fix an urgent issue.' As a Digital Detective, spot the manipulation tactics being used.

questions.question: What tactic is the caller using?
questions.hint: Attackers often pose as trusted figures like IT staff.

questions.question: What should you never share over an unsolicited call?
questions.hint: Sensitive credentials should never be given verbally.

questions.question: What is the best response to a suspicious caller?
options: Provide info if they sound confident
questions.hint: Always independently confirm identity before sharing anything.

title: Malware & Ransomware
subtitle: Understand how malicious software spreads and how to defend against it.
scenario: An employee downloads a 'free' software tool from an unofficial website. Shortly after, files on their computer become encrypted with a ransom note demanding payment. As a Digital Detective, trace how the infection happened and stop it from spreading.

questions.question: Where is it safest to download software from?
options: Official vendor websites or app stores
questions.hint: Stick to verified, trusted sources.

questions.question: What does ransomware typically do?
questions.hint: Think about what 'ransom' implies.

questions.question: What is the first step after suspecting infection?
questions.hint: Isolating the device prevents further spread.

title: Public Wi-Fi Safety
subtitle: Learn the risks of connecting to unsecured public networks.
scenario: An employee connects to free airport Wi-Fi to check work email without using a VPN. A nearby attacker is intercepting unencrypted traffic on the same network. As a Digital Detective, identify the risks and secure the connection.

questions.question: What tool helps protect data on public Wi-Fi?
questions.hint: It encrypts your traffic over untrusted networks.

questions.question: What should you avoid doing on public Wi-Fi?
questions.hint: Sensitive logins are riskiest on open networks.

questions.question: What is an 'evil twin' network?
options: A fake hotspot mimicking a real one
questions.hint: Attackers often clone legitimate network names.

title: Social Media Privacy
subtitle: Protect your personal information from oversharing online.
scenario: An employee's public social media posts reveal their job title, workplace, and daily routine, which an attacker uses to craft a convincing targeted phishing email. As a Digital Detective, identify the oversharing risks.

questions.question: What information should you limit sharing publicly?
questions.hint: Attackers use personal details to build targeted attacks.

questions.question: What is this targeted attack technique called?
questions.hint: It's phishing tailored to a specific individual.

questions.question: What should you regularly review on social platforms?
questions.hint: Settings control who can see your information.

title: Two-Factor Authentication
subtitle: Understand why extra verification steps matter for account security.
scenario: An attacker obtains an employee's password through a data breach but is blocked from logging in because a second verification step is required. As a Digital Detective, explore how this extra layer stopped the attack.

questions.question: What does two-factor authentication add?
options: A second verification step beyond the password
questions.hint: It combines something you know with something you have.

questions.question: Which is a common second factor?
options: A code from an authenticator app
questions.hint: Think of apps that generate time-based codes.

questions.question: What should you do if you get repeated unexpected MFA prompts?
options: Deny them and report the activity
questions.hint: Unexpected prompts may signal someone else has your password.

title: Safe Online Shopping
subtitle: Spot fake stores and protect your payment information online.
scenario: An employee finds an unbelievably cheap deal on a website with no contact information and an insecure checkout page. As a Digital Detective, determine if this store is legitimate.

questions.question: What is a red flag for a fake online store?
options: No contact info or secure checkout
A padlock icon in the browser
questions.hint: Legitimate stores provide transparency and security.

questions.question: What should you check before entering payment details?
questions.hint: Look for the padlock and 'https' in the address bar.

questions.question: What payment method offers more fraud protection?
questions.hint: Some payment methods are much easier to reverse if fraud occurs.

title: Identity Theft
subtitle: Learn how personal data is stolen and misused, and how to respond.
scenario: An employee notices unfamiliar accounts opened in their name after their personal data was exposed in a breach. As a Digital Detective, trace the source and help them respond.

questions.question: What is a common sign of identity theft?
questions.hint: Watch for activity you didn't authorize.

questions.question: What should you do first after suspecting identity theft?
options: Report it and monitor your accounts
questions.hint: Quick reporting limits further damage.

questions.question: What helps detect identity theft early?
questions.hint: Ongoing monitoring catches suspicious activity fast.

title: USB & Removable Media Safety
subtitle: Understand the hidden dangers of unknown USB drives and devices.
scenario: An employee finds a USB drive in the parking lot and plugs it into their work computer out of curiosity. As a Digital Detective, uncover why this is dangerous.

questions.question: Why is plugging in an unknown USB risky?
questions.hint: Unknown devices can be pre-loaded with malicious software.

questions.question: What should you do with a found USB drive?
options: Plug it in to see what's on it
questions.hint: Let security professionals examine it safely.

questions.question: What is this tactic of leaving infected drives called?
questions.hint: Attackers 'drop' devices hoping curiosity takes over.

title: Mobile Device Security
subtitle: Secure smartphones and tablets against common mobile threats.
scenario: An employee installs an app from an unofficial store that requests excessive permissions, secretly accessing contacts and messages. As a Digital Detective, identify the risky behavior.

questions.question: What should you check before installing an app?
questions.hint: Review what access an app actually needs.

questions.question: Where should mobile apps be downloaded from?
questions.hint: Official stores vet apps for security.

questions.question: What helps protect data if a phone is lost?
questions.hint: Security features can lock or erase data remotely.

title: Data Backup & Recovery
subtitle: Learn why regular backups are essential for resilience against attacks.
scenario: After a ransomware attack encrypts company files, an employee realizes there are no recent backups available, making recovery nearly impossible. As a Digital Detective, evaluate the backup strategy failure.

questions.question: Why are regular backups important?
options: They allow recovery without paying ransom
questions.hint: Backups are your safety net against data loss.

questions.question: Where should backups ideally be stored?
options: Offline or in a separate secure location
questions.hint: Isolated backups can't be encrypted by the same attack.

questions.question: What does the 3-2-1 backup rule recommend?
questions.hint: It's a well-known best practice for data redundancy.

title: Insider Threats
subtitle: Understand risks that come from within an organization.
scenario: A disgruntled employee about to leave the company downloads sensitive customer data onto a personal drive before their last day. As a Digital Detective, identify the insider threat indicators.

questions.question: What is a warning sign of insider threat activity?
questions.hint: Look for abnormal access or data transfer patterns.

questions.question: What control helps limit insider risk?
options: Access based on job role (least privilege)
questions.hint: Restricting access reduces potential damage.

questions.question: What should happen immediately when an employee leaves?
options: Wait a week to revoke access
questions.hint: Prompt deprovisioning prevents unauthorized access.

title: Physical Security & Tailgating
subtitle: Recognize how attackers exploit physical access to secure areas.
scenario: A stranger without a badge follows closely behind an employee through a secured office door, relying on politeness to gain entry. As a Digital Detective, identify this physical security breach.

questions.question: What is this technique called?
questions.hint: It involves following someone through a secured entry point.

questions.question: What should you do if a stranger tries to follow you in?
options: Politely ask them to badge in separately
questions.hint: Everyone should use their own access credentials.

questions.question: What helps prevent unauthorized physical access?
questions.hint: Formal visitor tracking improves accountability.

title: Cloud Security Basics
subtitle: Learn how to keep cloud-stored data and accounts protected.
scenario: An employee sets a shared cloud folder containing sensitive files to 'anyone with the link can view,' unintentionally exposing it to the public. As a Digital Detective, identify the misconfiguration.

questions.question: What caused the data exposure?
questions.hint: Sharing settings determine who can access files.

questions.question: What is a best practice for cloud sharing?
options: Share only with specific people who need access
questions.hint: Limit access to only those who require it.

questions.question: What extra protection secures cloud accounts?
questions.hint: Extra verification steps protect account access.

title: IoT Device Security
subtitle: Secure smart devices that connect to home and office networks.
scenario: A smart office camera is left with its default factory password, allowing an attacker to access the live feed remotely. As a Digital Detective, identify the vulnerability.

questions.question: What is the main vulnerability here?
questions.hint: Default credentials are widely known and easily guessed.

questions.question: What should you do when setting up a new IoT device?
questions.hint: Always customize security settings out of the box.

questions.question: What helps isolate risky IoT devices?
options: Putting them on a separate network segment
Connecting them to the main office network
questions.hint: Segmentation limits damage if a device is compromised.

title: Cyberbullying & Online Harassment
subtitle: Understand how to recognize, respond to, and prevent online harassment.
scenario: A colleague receives a series of threatening and abusive messages from an anonymous account on a workplace chat platform. As a Digital Detective, help identify how to respond appropriately.

questions.question: What should you do first if you receive harassing messages?
options: Ignore it and hope it stops
questions.hint: Documentation helps when reporting to authorities or platforms.

questions.question: Who should workplace harassment be reported to?
questions.hint: Organizations typically have formal reporting procedures.

questions.question: What platform feature can help stop ongoing harassment?
questions.hint: Most platforms offer built-in safety tools.

title: Safe Software Downloads
subtitle: Avoid trojanized software and verify authenticity before installing.
scenario: An employee searches for a free version of paid software and downloads a 'cracked' installer from a third-party site, which secretly bundles malware. As a Digital Detective, identify the danger signs.

questions.question: Why are cracked software downloads dangerous?
questions.hint: Pirated software is a common malware distribution method.

questions.question: What should you verify before installing software?
options: How many ads are on the page
questions.hint: Legitimate publishers sign their software.

questions.question: What can confirm a downloaded file hasn't been tampered with?
questions.hint: Hashes provide a unique fingerprint for file integrity.

title: Business Email Compromise
subtitle: Detect sophisticated email fraud targeting financial transactions.
scenario: A finance employee receives an email appearing to be from the CEO urgently requesting a wire transfer to a new vendor account, bypassing normal approval processes. As a Digital Detective, uncover the fraud.

questions.question: What is a major red flag in this scenario?
options: The email being sent during work hours
questions.hint: Fraudsters pressure victims to skip verification procedures.

questions.question: What should the employee do before transferring funds?
options: Verify the request through a separate trusted channel
Send the money quickly to help the CEO
Reply to the same email asking questions
Forward it to a coworker to decide
questions.hint: Always confirm large requests outside of email, like by phone.

questions.question: What technique do BEC attackers often use with email addresses?
options: Sending from personal Gmail with full disclosure
Using no email address at all
questions.hint: A single changed letter can make a domain look legitimate at a glance.

title: Incident Reporting & Response
subtitle: Learn the importance of quickly reporting and responding to security incidents.
scenario: An employee accidentally clicks a phishing link and enters their credentials, but hesitates to report it out of fear of getting in trouble. As a Digital Detective, explain why fast reporting matters.

questions.question: Why is it important to report incidents immediately?
options: It limits damage and enables a fast response
It's only necessary for major breaches
questions.hint: Quick action can stop an attack from spreading further.

questions.question: What should happen after credentials are compromised?
options: Change the password and report it immediately
Wait to see if anything bad happens
questions.hint: Fast credential resets reduce the attacker's window of access.

questions.question: What encourages employees to report mistakes without fear?
questions.hint: People report issues faster when they aren't afraid of blame.
